error.signature: RuntimeError: Error(s) in loading state_dict: Missing key(s) in state_dict: "layer4.weight". Unexpected key(s): "module.layer4.weight"

verdict.summary: State dict keys do not match model architecture. Common causes: DataParallel 'module.' prefix, renamed layers, or loading weights from a different model version.

dead_ends.action: Use strict=False to ignore all mismatched keys
dead_ends.why_fails: strict=False silently skips missing/unexpected keys; model runs with random weights in those layers, producing garbage output

dead_ends.action: Manually rename every key in the checkpoint
dead_ends.why_fails: Brittle and error-prone; breaks when the model changes. Fix the root cause instead.

workarounds.action: Strip 'module.' prefix from DataParallel state dict
workarounds.how: state_dict = {k.replace('module.', ''): v for k, v in checkpoint['state_dict'].items()}; model.load_state_dict(state_dict)

workarounds.action: Save and load state_dict from the unwrapped model
workarounds.how: torch.save(model.module.state_dict(), path)  # for DataParallel; or model.state_dict() for single GPU

workarounds.action: Compare keys to diagnose the mismatch
workarounds.how: model_keys = set(model.state_dict().keys()); ckpt_keys = set(ckpt.keys()); print('Missing:', model_keys - ckpt_keys)
